meta.title: Japan Working Holiday — Insider Living Hacks (Seattle → Tokyo)
meta.subtitle: One year. Tokyo. Synths, arcades, shipping code at 2am JST — with the paperwork that keeps me here. Land NRT 2026-06-30.

timeSensitive.item: Collect Residence Card (在留カード) at Narita immigration
timeSensitive.timing: On landing — issued at the NRT counter
timeSensitive.action: Present passport with WHV visa; the officer prints the card on the spot. Back is blank until you register an address. Carry it at all times — it is your legal ID.

timeSensitive.item: Carry proof-of-funds + status packet through immigration
timeSensitive.action: Keep ON YOUR PERSON (not checked luggage): passport with WHV sticker, fresh bank statement clearing ~CAD 3,500 (with return ticket) / ~CAD 4,500 (without), accommodation booking, onward/return ticket. Immigration can spot-check.

timeSensitive.item: Withdraw first JPY at a Seven Bank (7-Eleven) ATM in NRT arrivals
timeSensitive.timing: On landing, before leaving the airport
timeSensitive.action: Use a foreign card at Seven Bank for near-mid-market rates; skip the airport exchange counters. Carry cash — many small shops are cash-only.

timeSensitive.item: File Moving-In Notification (転入届) + register address at ward office
timeSensitive.timing: Within 14 days of settling in — HARD legal deadline
timeSensitive.action: Bring passport + Residence Card. They print your address on the card's back and create your juminhyo. Request 2-3 juminhyo copies (~¥300 each) for the bank. Missing the window risks a fine up to ¥200,000 under the Immigration Control Act (and up to ¥50,000 under the Basic Resident Registration Act), and in serious cases revocation of your status of residence after 90 days. This unlocks pension, NHI, My Number, banking.

timeSensitive.item: Enroll in National Pension (国民年金, Category-1) + apply for exemption (免除)
timeSensitive.timing: Same ward visit as address registration
timeSensitive.action: Legally mandatory for 20-59 residents regardless of visa. Do NOT assume the Canada-Japan totalization treaty exempts you — that only covers employer-detached workers. With zero prior-year Japan income you almost certainly qualify for full/partial exemption; enroll AND file menjo in one motion to pause the ~¥17k/month bill while staying compliant.

timeSensitive.item: Enroll in National Health Insurance (国民健康保険 / NHI)
timeSensitive.timing: Same ward visit (within 14 days of eligibility)
timeSensitive.action: Mandatory for any 3+ month visa holder. Bring Residence Card + passport. Coverage backdates to your move-in date, so prompt enrollment avoids a retroactive lump bill.

timeSensitive.item: Watch mailbox for My Number (個人番号) notification letter
timeSensitive.action: Assigned automatically when you register your address; letter arrives at your registered address. It is the key ID for insurance, tax, employment. Optionally apply for the physical My Number Card afterward (ready in ~2-4 weeks).

timeSensitive.item: Open a Japanese bank account (Japan Post Bank / Yucho first)
timeSensitive.timing: After address is printed on Residence Card
timeSensitive.action: Yucho doesn't strictly enforce the 6-month-residency rule megabanks impose, so WHV holders can open early. Bring Residence Card (with printed address) + juminhyo + Japanese phone number. No hanko needed in 2026 — signature accepted. This is the account wages/rent flow through.

timeSensitive.item: Get a Japanese phone number (passport-only SIM)
timeSensitive.timing: First week — gating dependency for all money apps
timeSensitive.action: PayPay, bank apps, and point programs need a Japanese number for SMS verification. Use Mobal or Sakura Mobile (passport-only, no residence card/address required) to break the SIM/bank/address chicken-and-egg. Order before departure or pick up at NRT.

timeSensitive.item: No tax-free shopping on a Working Holiday visa — you pay the 10%
timeSensitive.action: Japan's tax-free (consumption-tax-exempt) shopping is ONLY for short-term 'temporary visitor' status. A Working Holiday visa admits you with a residence status, so you are NOT eligible — not even on landing day. Budget the full 10% consumption tax on gear/electronics; don't delay big purchases for a tax-free window that doesn't exist for you.

timeSensitive.item: Re-entry permit BEFORE any trip outside Japan
timeSensitive.action: The Canadian WHV is single-entry — leaving without a re-entry permit VOIDS your status and strands you abroad. Tick the "special re-entry permit" box on the ED card at the airport (free, valid up to 1 year, must return before status expiry). No exceptions, even for a weekend in Korea.

timeSensitive.item: Appoint a tax agent (納税管理人) BEFORE you leave Japan
timeSensitive.action: To reclaim the 20.42% withheld on a pension lump-sum AND to settle year-2 residence tax (levied on Jan-1-2027 residency, can bill after you go), appoint a Tax Payment Administrator at the ward tax office before leaving. Without it you forfeit the refund. Lump-sum claimable within 2 years of departure. Note: the 20.42% withholding applies to the Employees' Pension (厚生年金) lump-sum; if you only ever pay National Pension (国民年金, Cat-1) the lump-sum has no income-tax withholding. The lump-sum cap rose to 96 contribution months (8 yrs) for departures on/after 1 Apr 2026 (was 60) — rates for months 61+ still pending; verify closer.

bookByTimeline.what: Sumida River Fireworks Festival (Jul 25) — paid grandstand/yakatabune boat seats
bookByTimeline.action: Official paid grandstand + yakatabune boat-seat applications for the Jul 25 show typically OPEN in June — apply NOW via the official festival site (first-come + postcard lottery). Boat seats ~¥31,000–39,600. If sold out, scout a free riverside spot and arrive hours early.

DONE — booked Sakura House (Makoto) through Jul 7; find long-term housing before checkout. (Original guidance:) BOOK NOW — already overdue per plan (accommodation due 2026-06-20, share house apply 2026-06-22). Reserve 5-10 nights near a hub (Shinjuku/Shibuya/Ueno) and apply online to Oakhouse/Sakura House/Borderless House before you fly.

bookByTimeline.what: Pre-departure SIM/eSIM + Wise card (home-issued, optional) + Mobile Suica
bookByTimeline.action: Order Wise card (optional, Chase-Sapphire-only setup) and verify before flying while in the US (a Japan-issued Wise card loses Apple/Google Pay). Install Ubigi/Sakura eSIM un-activated; pre-order Mobal SIM. Set up Mobile Suica in Apple Wallet. FLAG: Wise due 2026-06-29, eSIM install by 2026-06-29.

bookByTimeline.what: teamLab Planets TOKYO — Summer Sunflower Exclusive (event Jul 1)
bookByTimeline.action: Book advance timed tickets via DMM/Klook ~2-3 weeks ahead; booking window opens ~2026-06-28. FLAG: this lands on your arrival-week — buy as soon as the slot calendar opens.

Lawson Ticket only, released 10:00 JST on the 10th for the FOLLOWING month. Set an alarm for 09:55 JST on 2026-07-10 (buy Aug), 2026-08-10 (buy Sep), etc. Ticket carries lead visitor's passport name; ID checked at entry. Slots: 10/12/14/16.

bookByTimeline.what: Ghibli Park (Aichi) — overnight trip, September tickets
bookByTimeline.action: Released 14:00 JST on the 10th, TWO months ahead (Jun 10 = Aug, Jul 10 = Sep). All advance reservation, no door sales. Avoid the 27th (Aichi-resident priority). Use Klook/KKday/JTB for English + foreign card, or official once you have a JP address + card.
Wristband/early-entry and circle-catalog logistics open ~2026-07-15. Buy the catalog/wristband; plan Big Sight access, cash, heat gear. C108 overlaps Obon/Asakusa Toro Nagashi — pick your day.
Advance day-tickets open ~2026-07-20. Buy online to skip same-day lines at Makuhari Messe.

bookByTimeline.what: Tokyo Disney — first quiet-weekday visit (target Tue/Wed/Thu late Jul)
bookByTimeline.action: Online ticket window opens ~60 days ahead at 14:00 JST. Set up the TDR app FIRST. For guaranteed Fantasy Springs rides, book a Vacation Package months ahead (reserve.tokyodisneyresort.jp) — those sell out. Otherwise rope-drop + free Standby Pass + DPA only on the 3 Fantasy Springs headliners + Soaring. DisneySea 25th 'Sparkling Jubilee' runs through Mar 2027.
Tickets on sale ~2026-08-08 via official Ticket Oosumo or buysumotickets.com. They sell fast — buy the morning they release.

bookByTimeline.what: Maker Faire Tokyo 2026 (Sep 5) + Tokyo Game Show 2026 (Sep 17-21)
bookByTimeline.action: Maker Faire advance tickets ~2026-09-04; TGS public-day tickets via official site ~2026-09-10. FLAG TGS 30th anniversary expanded 5-day — buy business-day vs public-day deliberately; public days are packed.

bookByTimeline.what: Tokyo Disney New Year's Program (Jan 1) + Fukubukuro lucky bags
bookByTimeline.action: Disney hotel reservations open 11:00 JST four months prior (~Sep 1 for New Year); Vacation Packages 5–6 months ahead. The NYE countdown park entry is a SEPARATE earlier lottery that sells out instantly — watch for its lottery announcement. Set alarms to the exact JST minute.

bookByTimeline.what: Nikko / Oku-Nikko koyo peak (Oct ~15) — limited-express seats
bookByTimeline.action: Reserve Tobu SPACIA X / Revaty Kegon seats in advance for foliage season; get the Tobu 'Nikko Pass All Area' (~¥4,780). Irohazaka gridlocks at peak — go midweek, leave Asakusa 7-8am.
C109 catalog/wristband logistics ~2026-11-30; Jump Festa tickets ~2026-11-30. FLAG C109 is a special 3-day edition spanning NYE-adjacent dates — plan around Hatsumode and Disney NYE program.

ONLY if you'll owe Japanese resident/income tax and are registered as a resident on Jan 1, 2027. With little/no 2026 Japan income there's nothing to offset — skip it. Otherwise donations must complete by 2026-12-31.

bookByTimeline.what: GALA Yuzawa ski day-trip (Jan ~9) — Shinkansen + lift package
bookByTimeline.action: Reserve JR EAST 'GALA Yuzawa' Shinkansen+lift+rental package ~1 week ahead via JR East online. Book early-Jan weekday to dodge crowds.
Book any GW domestic travel/Shinkansen by late Feb 2027 — peak price/crowd surge. Ashikaga Flower Park wisteria timed tickets open ~2027-04-25 window; buy early for the illuminated evening slot.

bookByTimeline.what: Hanami: Shinjuku Gyoen reserved/paid entry + Chidorigafuchi rowboats (peak ~Mar 29)
bookByTimeline.action: Shinjuku Gyoen timed entry reservations open ~2027-03-20; Meguro River/Chidorigafuchi viewing fills fast. FLAG bloom dates are 'low' confidence — verify the official kaika/mankai forecast in early March and book the moment slots release.

calendar.title: Sakura House (Makoto) — initial stay
calendar.area: Tokyo (Sakura House Makoto)
calendar.bookingNotes: Initial accommodation, arrival through Jul 7. Verify the exact property name (Makoto) against your booking confirmation — Sakura House has many named buildings. Find long-term housing before checkout.

calendar.title: Forest Beer Garden (Mori no Beer Garden), Meiji Jingu Gaien
calendar.area: Aoyama-itchome / Shinjuku (Meiji Jingu Gaien, in the trees)
calendar.cost: All-you-can-eat BBQ + all-you-can-drink plans from ~¥5,980 (2hr)
calendar.bookingNotes: 1,000 seats among the gingko trees; open ~Apr 22–Sep 30, 2026. Reserve online for weekends/groups (walk-up possible weekdays). Great default hangout spot all summer for meetups; cash or card. Open the whole window of your arrival onward.
calendar.why: Tokyo's most famous beer garden — a reliable, social, all-budgets summer venue ideal for organizing dev/music meetups.

calendar.title: Hilton Tokyo 'Sunset Beach Australia' Rooftop Beer Garden
calendar.area: Nishi-Shinjuku (Hilton Tokyo 7F terrace, near Tochomae / Shinjuku Stn)
calendar.cost: ~¥7,000 pp for a 2-hour free-flow + Aussie platter package
calendar.bookingNotes: Returns ~May 15–Sep 30, 2026 (verify exact dates). Reserve online; popular on weekends. Higher-end splurge option vs. Forest Beer Garden — good for a special night with a Tokyo skyline view.
calendar.why: Rooftop variety for your 'food across all budgets' goal — a polished splurge beer garden to balance the free street festivals.

calendar.title: Adachi Fireworks Festival (48th) — NOTE: now late-May, just before your window
calendar.area: Adachi Ward (Arakawa River, between Chiyoda-line bridge & Nishiarai Bridge; Kita-Senju Stn)
calendar.cost: Free on riverbank; paid seating available (often sold out by mid-May)
calendar.bookingNotes: HEADS-UP: moved permanently to MAY to avoid heatstroke — Sat May 30, 2026, 19:20–20:20, ~13,000 shells incl. the 250m 'Double Niagara.' This is BEFORE your Jun 30 arrival, so you'll miss 2026 — included so you know it's no longer a July/August option. Catch it in May 2027 instead. ⚠️ Typhoon/rain can postpone or cancel — check the official site the day before.
calendar.why: You asked for Adachi specifically; flagging that it has shifted out of the summer window so you don't plan around it for 2026.

calendar.title: Mt. Takao Beer Mount (mountainside beer garden)
calendar.area: Hachioji / western Tokyo (Mt. Takao, ~halfway up via cable car from Takaosanguchi Stn)
calendar.cost: All-you-can-eat/drink ~¥3,800–4,000 (2hr); + cable car fare ~¥490 each way
calendar.bookingNotes: Seasonal open-air beer garden with a panoramic view over the Kanto plain; typically mid-June to mid-October (verify 2026 dates on official Takaosan site). Pair with an easy day hike. Walk-up + online reservation.
calendar.why: Combines a cheap nature day-trip with a beer garden and city views — a refreshing escape from summer Tokyo heat.

calendar.title: WHV admin window — register address, NHI, pension, My Number (first 14 days)
calendar.area: Your local ward office, Tokyo
calendar.cost: NHI ~JPY 1,500–3,000/mo first year; National Pension ~JPY 17,000/mo (low-income exemption or post-departure lump-sum refund possible — keep receipts).
calendar.bookingNotes: Bring passport + residence card + proof of address. Ask the ward office about pension EXEMPTION while on WHV (low income) and the lump-sum withdrawal for when you leave. File dattai ichijikin within 2 years of leaving Japan. 14-day rule and enrollment are fixed law; confirm pension lump-sum specifics at your ward office / JPS.
calendar.why: Not autumn itself, but a Canadian-WHV gotcha that frees up your autumn: register your address at the ward office within 14 days of arrival (residence card issued at NRT on entry). Same office: enroll in National Health Insurance (~JPY 1,500–3,000/mo year 1) and National Pension (mandatory for residents 20–59 staying 3+ months). Settle this in summer so autumn is free. As a Canadian leaving within ~12 months, plan a Pension Lump-sum Withdrawal (dattai ichijikin) after departure and keep tax/proof-of-funds records.

calendar.title: teamLab Planets TOKYO — Summer Sunflower Exclusive
calendar.area: Toyosu / Koto (Shin-Toyosu Stn, Yurikamome line)
calendar.cost: ~¥3,800–4,800 adult timed ticket (2026 pricing; verify)
calendar.bookingNotes: Buy timed-entry tickets online in advance — slots sell out on weekends and during summer. The 'Koi and People' water artwork transforms into blooming sunflowers Jul 1–31 only (date confirmed via teamLab summer release; verify exact 2026 window closer). Barefoot/water-immersion — wear shorts. Venue runs through end of 2027.
calendar.why: Direct hit on your anime/tech-culture + seasonal interests; the sunflower transformation is a July-only digital-art event.

calendar.title: Retro game & synth hunting (ongoing, not a single dated event)
calendar.area: Akihabara (Super Potato, Mandarake, Hard Off) + Ohi Racecourse flea market, Tokyo
calendar.cost: Free to browse; flea-market items from ~JPY 100. Ohi Keibajo (Tokyo City Flea Market) charges no entry.
calendar.bookingNotes: No fixed dated 'swap meet' for retro games/synths was confirmed — instead treat this as a recurring activity. Ohi Racecourse flea market (Tokyo's largest, ~600 stalls) runs most weekends when no races — check the NANIKA Tokyo flea-market calendar for dates. For synth/gear swaps, follow Ableton Meetup Tokyo and modular/Eurorack community channels (RA, ModWiggler) for pop-up meets. Super Potato (Akihabara) is a permanent retro shop, walk-up.
calendar.why: Directly serves the retro-games + music-gear interests on a flexible schedule; flea markets and Akihabara shops are the realistic 'swap meet' substitute and run year-round, fitting around the dated conventions above.

calendar.title: Mitama Matsuri (30,000 Lantern Festival) at Yasukuni Shrine
calendar.area: Kudanshita / Chiyoda (Yasukuni Shrine, 5-min walk from Kudanshita Stn)
calendar.cost: Free admission; food/drink stalls cash-based
calendar.bookingNotes: Walk-up. No tickets. Lanterns light at 18:00 nightly. Mikoshi processions Jul 13 & 15 ~18:30; Aomori Nebuta + Awa Odori Jul 14 ~18:30. Goes early in your trip (you land Jun 30) — great low-cost first matsuri. Arrive before 18:00 to beat the 300k crowd. ⚠️ Typhoon/rain can postpone or cancel — check the official site the day before.
calendar.why: Tokyo's iconic Obon lantern-tunnel festival; matches your seasonal-experience + culture interests and is free.

calendar.title: Fuji Rock Festival '26
calendar.area: Naeba Ski Resort, Niigata (~2.5h from Tokyo by shinkansen+bus)
calendar.cost: ~¥25,000/day to ~¥55,000 3-day; camping extra
calendar.bookingNotes: Fri–Sun, 24–26 Jul 2026 (3-day) · e+ / official site / Ticket Pia
calendar.why: Japan's biggest and best-curated festival — sprawling mountain site with a deep electronic/leftfield presence (Red Marquee, Field of Heaven) alongside indie and global headliners.

calendar.title: Sumida River Fireworks Festival (Sumidagawa Hanabi Taikai)
calendar.area: Asakusa / Sumida (two launch sites along the Sumida River near Asakusa & Skytree)
calendar.cost: Free along riverbanks (extremely crowded). Paid reserved seating ~¥7,000 (single chair) up to ~¥25,000 (5-person sheet)
calendar.bookingNotes: ~20,000 shells, 19:00–20:30, Sat Jul 25 (annual last-Sat-of-July pattern; near-confirmed). Paid 'citizen sponsorship' / reserved viewing typically opens early May 2026 and sells out fast — check official site (sumidagawa-hanabi.com) and Taito City in late April. For free viewing, claim a spot by mid-afternoon or watch from a side street / Skytree-area. Cancelled (not postponed) in storms.
calendar.why: The most famous Tokyo hanabi — bucket-list summer experience; flagged paid-seat lottery so you don't miss the May booking window.

calendar.title: Wonder Festival 2026 [Summer] (figures & garage kits)
calendar.area: Makuhari Messe, Chiba (Kaihimmakuhari, ~30 min from Tokyo Station)
calendar.cost: Advance ticket roughly JPY 2,500-3,500; the official Guide Book doubles as the entry ticket. Same-day (if available) higher.
calendar.bookingNotes: Buy the advance Guide Book / ticket via the official site or convenience-store kiosks before the event; the printed Guide Book is the entry pass and also unlocks 'one-day-only' licensed garage kits. One-day event (Sunday) 10:00-17:00. Verify exact ticket price closer.
calendar.why: Japan's biggest figure/garage-kit festival (~2,000 exhibitors) — prime for anime/figure collectors and a great early-window outing right after landing.

calendar.title: Edogawa Fireworks Festival (incl. world's tallest 'Fuji no Daikageki')
calendar.area: Edogawa Ward (Edogawa riverbank; also viewable Ichikawa side, Chiba). Nearest: Shinozaki / Koiwa Stn
calendar.cost: Free on riverbank; paid non-reserved ~¥2,000–4,000, reserved seats from ~¥5,000
calendar.bookingNotes: ~14,000 shells synced to music; 50+ year history. Aug 1 (Sat) is best estimate — verify on official Edogawa site closer. If you want a seat, buy reserved tickets when they release (typically June–July). Free riverbank viewing is generous here vs. Sumida. ⚠️ Typhoon/rain can postpone or cancel — check the official site the day before.
calendar.why: Huge, music-synced show with the signature opening barrage; a less brutally crowded free-viewing alternative to Sumida.

calendar.title: Meiji Jingu Gaien Fireworks Festival (45th)
calendar.area: Shinjuku/Aoyama (Jingu Stadium + Chichibunomiya Rugby Stadium; inside the Yamanote loop)
calendar.cost: All seats reserved & PAID. 2025 started ~¥7,000; 2026 prices TBA
calendar.bookingNotes: ~10,000 shells 19:30–20:30 with live performances first. Sat Aug 8 best estimate (verify). No free viewing — tickets required, sell out; buy as soon as they go on sale (typically June). Unusually, this one POSTPONES to Aug 9 in bad weather rather than cancelling.
calendar.why: The only major hanabi inside central Tokyo's Yamanote loop — easy access from anywhere you base, with a concert-style atmosphere.

calendar.title: Fukagawa Hachiman Matsuri (Mizu-kake Water-Throwing Festival) — Full Hon-Matsuri Year
calendar.area: Monzen-Nakacho / Koto (Tomioka Hachiman-gu Shrine; route near Kiba Park & Kiyosumi Garden)
calendar.cost: Free
calendar.bookingNotes: 2026 is a once-every-3-years FULL Hon-matsuri (2020 / 2023 / 2026) — 50+ giant mikoshi paraded while crowds douse carriers with purification water. Grand procession is Sun Aug 16 (~7:30 AM start from Tomioka Hachiman-gu). Festival window Aug 12–16; verify exact procession day closer. Expect to get soaked — that's the point. One of Tokyo's 'three great festivals.' ⚠️ Typhoon/rain can postpone or cancel — check the official site the day before.
calendar.why: Rare big-scale year you won't get again until 2029 — a soaking, high-energy matsuri; do NOT miss this in your one Tokyo summer.

calendar.title: Summer Sonic 2026 (Tokyo)
calendar.area: Chiba / Makuhari (ZOZO Marine + Makuhari Messe) — not Tokyo proper
calendar.cost: ~¥20,000/day; multi-day options
calendar.bookingNotes: Fri–Sun, 14–16 Aug 2026 (25th anniversary, first-ever 3-day) · e+ / official site
calendar.why: Urban festival with a strong pop/indie/rock bill and a dedicated dance stage (Sonic Stage / Sonicmania all-nighter) for electronic acts. Easy day-trip from central Tokyo.

calendar.title: Asakusa Toro Nagashi (Sumida River Lantern Floating, Obon)
calendar.area: Asakusa (Sumida River near Azuma Bridge, 5–6 min from Senso-ji / Kaminarimon)
calendar.cost: Free to watch; ~¥2,000 to float your own inscribed lantern (typical)
calendar.bookingNotes: ~2,500 lanterns floated for ancestral spirits during Obon. Held in mid-August (often a Saturday ~Aug 15); exact 2026 date not yet published — mark 'verify closer' via Asakusa Tourism Federation (e-asakusa.jp). Quiet, moving counterpoint to the fireworks.
calendar.why: Authentic Obon ritual right in central Tokyo; pairs your seasonal + culture interests with a reflective, low-cost evening.

calendar.title: Comiket C108 (Summer Comic Market)
calendar.area: Tokyo Big Sight, Ariake, Koto-ku, Tokyo
calendar.cost: Entry requires a paid wristband/ticket bought in advance via Comiket WebCatalog; tiered ~JPY 1,000-5,000+ depending on entry slot (earlier = pricier). A limited number sold same-day. WebCatalog itself ~JPY 1,000.
calendar.bookingNotes: CRITICAL booking: there is no free general entry anymore. You must buy a timed-entry wristband through the Comike WebCatalog app (circle.ms account). Pricing/schedule was published in late June 2026; Early-Entry tickets go on sale first, other tiers ~1 month before (mid-to-late July). Buy as soon as the sale opens — early-morning slots sell out. Foreigners can attend; official X/Facebook post English guidance. Arriving already in cosplay is banned (use the paid changing room). Verify exact ticket on-sale date and prices at comiket.co.jp closer to the event.
calendar.why: The world's largest doujinshi/fan-works convention and a cornerstone of anime/manga/games/tech-otaku culture; lands two weeks after arrival, an ideal first big event. Smaller than usual this round (East 4-6 halls under construction).

calendar.title: Tokyo Koenji Awa Odori (67th)
calendar.area: Koenji / Suginami (streets around JR Koenji & Shin-Koenji Stns)
calendar.cost: Free (street viewing); some grandstand seats may be paid/limited
calendar.bookingNotes: Sat–Sun Aug 29–30, ~17:00–20:00. 12,000+ dancers, 180 troupes, ~1M spectators — the biggest Awa Odori outside Tokushima. Walk-up; arrive early for street-front spots. Closes out your summer on a high. ⚠️ Typhoon/rain can postpone or cancel — check the official site the day before.
calendar.why: Massive, free, high-energy street-dance festival in a hip neighborhood full of vintage synth/record shops — perfect for your music-gear + meetup interests.

calendar.title: Maker Faire Tokyo 2026 (DIY / electronics / robotics / 3D printing)
calendar.area: Ariake GYM-EX (Jimedex), 1-10-1 Ariake, Koto-ku, Tokyo
calendar.cost: Advance: JPY 1,400 adult (early-bird JPY 1,000 ended Aug 2), JPY 500 under-18; evening-discount adult JPY 1,000. Same-day: JPY 1,800 adult, JPY 700 under-18.
calendar.bookingNotes: Advance tickets via eplus / Lawson Ticket (l-tike). Cheaper than door price; buy advance to save. Sat 12:00-18:00, Sun 10:00-17:00. No lottery — straightforward purchase.
calendar.why: Japan's largest maker/hardware-hacking festival — directly matches the build-software / synth-DIY / electronics-tinkering interests; great for meeting the Tokyo hardware/indie-dev community.

calendar.title: Tokyo Game Show 2026 (30th anniversary, expanded 5-day)
calendar.area: Makuhari Messe, Chiba
calendar.cost: Public-day ticket typically ~JPY 2,000-3,000 advance (timed entry); Business Days require trade registration. Free for under a certain age may apply — verify. Premium/early-entry tiers cost more.
calendar.bookingNotes: Public days are Sat-Mon Sep 19-21; Business Days Sep 17-18 (industry only — relevant if working in games/remote dev, register as trade). Public tickets are timed-entry and sell out — buy advance online as soon as sales open (usually ~early Aug). Verify final ticket price/on-sale date at the official TGS site.
calendar.why: Asia's premier video-game expo (~300,000 visitors, 3,500 booths) and a 30th-anniversary edition — core games/arcade interest plus strong networking for remote/game-dev work.

calendar.title: Ultra Japan 2026
calendar.area: Tokyo — Odaiba Ultra Park (Aomi/Daiba; Yurikamome or Rinkai Line)
calendar.bookingNotes: Sat–Sun, 19–20 Sep 2026 (2-day) · Official site / Ticket Pia
calendar.why: Big-stage EDM/house/techno festival in the city — mainstream and commercial, but a spectacle and an easy in-town electronic weekend.

calendar.title: electrox 2027 (indoor NYE EDM festival)
calendar.area: Makuhari Messe, Chiba (Kaihimmakuhari Stn, ~40 min from Tokyo Stn)
calendar.bookingNotes: Japan's marquee indoor New-Year EDM festival; line-up + exact 2026→27 dates drop ~autumn — verify closer on the official site.
calendar.why: Big-room/mainstage EDM under one roof to ring in the new year out of the cold — the indoor commercial counterpart to summer's Ultra.

calendar.title: Rainbow Disco Club (RDC) 2027
calendar.area: East Izu, Shizuoka — Tenshi-no-Sato outdoor park (~2.5–3 hr from Tokyo)
calendar.cost: ~¥18,000–28,000 multi-day + lodging/camping
calendar.bookingNotes: Golden Week (early May); house/techno/disco. Early-bird tickets sell out — verify 2027 dates closer.
calendar.why: Tokyo heads' favourite OUTDOOR house/techno/disco festival — deep, tasteful, scenic. The non-commercial counterpoint to Ultra.

calendar.title: S2O Japan Songkran Music Festival (water EDM)
calendar.area: Tokyo area — venue varies year to year (recent eds. around Odaiba); check official
calendar.bookingNotes: Thai-style water + EDM festival, typically early May — you WILL get soaked. Dates/venue vary — verify closer.
calendar.why: A soaking-wet big-stage EDM party — pure sunny spectacle if you want a ridiculous dance day.

calendar.title: FFKT 2026 (formerly Taicoclub)
calendar.area: Izu Shimoda, Shizuoka — Hotel Izukyu, Shirahama Beach (~3 hr via Odoriko ltd express)
calendar.cost: ~¥15,000–22,000 + lodging/camping
calendar.bookingNotes: Sat–Sun, 26–27 Sep 2026 (best estimate — verify closer) · Official site / RA / e+
calendar.why: Tastemaker outdoor festival blending forward-thinking electronic, ambient and live acts on a scenic seaside site. A core date for the discerning electronic crowd. Verify dates closer.

calendar.title: Blue Note Jazz Festival in Japan 2026
calendar.area: Tokyo — Ariake Arena, Koto (Yurikamome / Rinkai Line, Toyosu side)
calendar.bookingNotes: Sat–Sun, 27–28 Sep 2026 (2-day) · Official site / e+
calendar.why: Arena-scale jazz/soul/R&B festival from the Blue Note team — a concentrated dose of marquee jazz and soul-adjacent acts in one weekend. Verify dates closer.

calendar.title: The Labyrinth 2026 (Mindgames)
calendar.area: Minakami, Gunma — Hodaigi Camp Site (~2.5 hr; shuttle from parking)
calendar.cost: ~¥18,000–25,000 (camping festival)
calendar.bookingNotes: Sat–Mon, 10–12 Oct 2026 (3 nights camping) · Official site (limited capacity, sells out)
calendar.why: Legendary, intimate deep-/minimal-techno camping festival in the mountains with a famously pristine sound system and a no-frills, music-devotee ethos. A bucket-list rave. Verify dates closer.

calendar.title: Nikko / Oku-Nikko koyo peak — Irohazaka, Lake Chuzenji, Kegon Falls
calendar.area: Nikko (Tochigi) — ~2hr from Tokyo via Tobu Nikko Line
calendar.cost: Free to view; Tobu round-trip ~JPY 2,800–6,000 depending on pass. Nikko All Area / 2-Day pass recommended. Kegon Falls elevator JPY 570.
calendar.bookingNotes: Walk-up possible but Irohazaka and the Akechidaira ropeway get severely congested on autumn WEEKENDS — go midweek or leave Tokyo before 7am. Buy a Tobu Nikko Pass in advance; stay overnight to beat crowds. Annual pattern reliable; exact peak shifts with weather — verify closer via japan-guide koyo reports in early Oct.
calendar.why: Nikko peaks earliest in the region: high-elevation Oku-Nikko colors arrive early Oct, Lake Chuzenji and Kegon Falls peak roughly Oct 18–Nov 1, Irohazaka winding road mid-to-late Oct. Your single best EARLY autumn-leaves day trip from Tokyo before the city itself turns. Town/Shinkyo Bridge area peaks later (~Nov 5–12).

calendar.title: Yomiuriland Jewellumination (winter illumination opens)
calendar.area: Yomiuriland, Inagi/Kawasaki — Keio line + gondola or bus
calendar.cost: Illumination/'Night' ticket ~JPY 1,800–2,200; full park + pool/rides passes higher.
calendar.bookingNotes: Buy timed e-tickets online to skip the queue. Weekends busy; best on a clear evening. 2026 open date estimated from 2025 (Oct 23) — verify on official Yomiuriland site.
calendar.why: One of Tokyo's earliest and largest winter illuminations (~6.5 million LEDs) — opens late October, so a great early-autumn evening option pairing an amusement park with lights. 2025 season opened Oct 23; 2026 expected late Oct.

calendar.title: Mt. Takao Autumn Leaves (Momiji) Festival
calendar.area: Mt. Takao, Hachioji — ~50 min from Shinjuku on Keio Line
calendar.cost: Free festival/trails; Keio round-trip ~JPY 800; cable car/chairlift ~JPY 490 one-way / JPY 950 round.
calendar.bookingNotes: No booking. AVOID mid-to-late-Nov weekends — cable car queues can exceed 1–2 hours. Go weekday morning. Trail 1 is paved; bring layers. ⚠️ Typhoon/rain can postpone or cancel — check the official site the day before.
calendar.why: Tokyo's most accessible mountain foliage and a Michelin 3-star natural site. Runs late Oct–mid Dec with performances at the Kiyotaki cable car base; peak color mid-Nov at summit, late Nov at base. Perfect cheap, half-day hike + leaves combo from Tokyo.

calendar.title: Shibuya Halloween — AVOID / know the rules (street drinking ban, Hachiko walled off)
calendar.area: Shibuya, Tokyo
calendar.cost: Free (but fines/heavy police presence for street drinking). Club/venue Halloween events typically JPY 2,500–5,000.
calendar.bookingNotes: If you want a night out, book a ticketed event (club in Shinjuku/Roppongi or a costume party) in advance — Shibuya streets are a no-go. Carry your residence card. Rules are established annual policy since 2024; exact 2026 wall dates verify closer to late Oct.
calendar.why: Shibuya Ward actively discourages Halloween gatherings. Expect: year-round public street-drinking ban enforced strictly 6pm–5am on Halloween; ~58 nearby shops halt alcohol sales; traffic restrictions near the Scramble from ~4pm Oct 31; Hachiko statue walled off Oct 30–Nov 1; Luup scooter ports suspended. Officials explicitly ask tourists NOT to gather. Treat as a 'where NOT to be' — go to a ticketed venue/club or another ward instead.

calendar.title: Kawaguchiko Autumn Leaves Festival & Momiji Corridor (Mt. Fuji views)
calendar.area: Lake Kawaguchiko, Yamanashi — ~2hr from Shinjuku (bus/Fuji Excursion)
calendar.cost: Free festival/corridor; Fuji Excursion train or highway bus round-trip ~JPY 4,000–5,800.
calendar.bookingNotes: Reserve the Shinjuku highway bus or Fuji Excursion seat in advance for peak-color weekends — they sell out. Weekday strongly advised. Clear mornings give the best Fuji reflection. Peak dates weather-dependent — verify closer. ⚠️ Typhoon/rain can postpone or cancel — check the official site the day before.
calendar.why: The Momiji Kairo (Maple Corridor) — a 150m tunnel of ~60 maples along a canal near the Itchiku Kubota Art Museum — lit nightly from ~4:30pm, framed by Mt. Fuji. Iconic autumn day-trip. Festival runs all Nov; color peaks ~Nov 10–20.

calendar.title: Roppongi Hills Keyakizaka-dori Illumination (blue LEDs + Tokyo Tower)
calendar.area: Roppongi
calendar.cost: Free (street); paid Tokyo City View observation deck optional
calendar.bookingNotes: Walk-up. ~5pm-11pm. ~400m street, ~800,000 blue/white LEDs framing Tokyo Tower. Also a Christmas Market in the Roppongi Hills arena (food/gluhwein) typically late Nov-Dec 25. DATE NOTE: 2025 ran Nov 4 - Dec 25; 2026 estimate mirrors this. Ends Dec 25 every year - don't push to January. Verify closer.
calendar.why: The iconic blue-lights-with-Tokyo-Tower shot. Short season (ends Christmas Day). Central to Roppongi nightlife/meetups and strong for tech/photo culture.

calendar.title: Tori-no-Ichi (Ichi-no-Tori, First Rooster) — Otori Shrine, Asakusa
calendar.area: Otori Shrine & Chokoku-ji, Asakusa/Senzoku, Taito
calendar.cost: Free entry; small kumade from ~JPY 1,000, larger ones JPY 3,000–10,000+ (clapping ritual included).
calendar.bookingNotes: No booking. Goes late into the night; busiest after dark. Buying a kumade is traditional for those starting work/business in Japan — fitting for your new chapter. ⚠️ Typhoon/rain can postpone or cancel — check the official site the day before.
calendar.why: Edo-era good-fortune fair held on each 'rooster day' in November. Hundreds of stalls sell ornate kumade (lucky rakes that 'rake in' prosperity), open midnight-to-midnight. Atmospheric, very local, free to wander — a quintessential Tokyo autumn night. 2026 rooster days: Nov 7 (Ichi), Nov 19 (Ni); no San-no-Tori this year.

calendar.title: Marunouchi Illumination (Naka-dori champagne-gold lights)
calendar.area: Marunouchi / Tokyo Station
calendar.cost: Free
calendar.bookingNotes: Walk-up, no ticket. Best 16:00-23:00; extended to midnight in December. Pair with the Gyoko-dori/Tokyo Station front avenue (~250 trees) which usually runs a shorter window mid-Nov to ~Dec 25. DATE NOTE: 2025-26 ran Nov 13 - Feb 15; 2026-27 not yet officially posted, estimate mirrors annual pattern. Verify closer (announced ~Oct 2026).
calendar.why: Tokyo's most elegant, free illumination right by Tokyo Station. Signature champagne-gold lights line Naka-dori ~1.2km. Easy after-work stroll, no-cost winter staple for a Tokyo resident.

calendar.title: Showa Kinen Park koyo peak + Autumn Night Illumination
calendar.area: Showa Kinen Park, Tachikawa — ~30 min from Shinjuku (JR Chuo to Nishi-Tachikawa)
calendar.cost: Park entry JPY 450 adult; daytime walk-in. Evening illumination may need a separate timed ticket (~JPY 300–1,000 recent years).
calendar.bookingNotes: Check the official site for the night-illumination ticketing window — recent years used timed reservations; secure one a few days ahead for peak weekends. Peak/illumination dates estimated from prior years — verify closer.
calendar.why: Tokyo's biggest accessible foliage park: two 200–300m ginkgo boulevards (98+ trees), maple groves, a Japanese garden. Evening light-ups (~4:30–8:30pm) of the ginkgo avenue and garden during the late-Nov peak. Spacious — less crushing than central spots. Ginkgo peaks mid-Nov, maples late Nov.

calendar.title: Jingu Gaien Ginkgo Festival (Icho Matsuri) — 300m golden tunnel + food stalls
calendar.area: Meiji Jingu Gaien, Aoyama, Minato
calendar.cost: Free; food stalls JPY 500–1,200 per item.
calendar.bookingNotes: No booking. Very crowded on late-Nov weekends; visit on a weekday around peak. Best gold ~Nov 20–Dec 5. Festival window and light-up dates estimated from prior years — verify closer. ⚠️ Typhoon/rain can postpone or cancel — check the official site the day before.
calendar.why: Tokyo's most famous ginkgo avenue — a 300m double row forming a golden canopy. ~32 regional food stalls (great cheap-eats spread). Trees lit ~4:30–7:30pm in the final stretch (approx Nov 23–Dec 1). Central, free, photogenic — a must-do urban koyo.

calendar.title: Design Festa Vol.64 (Autumn) — independent art & craft fair
calendar.area: Tokyo Big Sight, Ariake, Koto-ku, Tokyo
calendar.cost: Advance: JPY 800 one-day / JPY 1,500 two-day. Same-day: JPY 1,000 one-day (no same-day 2-day pass).
calendar.bookingNotes: Tickets via Design Festa Office Gallery (Harajuku), online ticketing, or convenience stores. No lottery. Confirm Vol.64 exact dates closer — Nov 15-16 reported but verify on the official Design Festa site.
calendar.why: One of Asia's largest independent art/craft events (6,500+ booths) — overlaps maker, illustration, music-merch and indie-creator scenes; cheap and easy to attend.

calendar.title: Tori-no-Ichi (Ni-no-Tori, Second Rooster) — Otori Shrine, Asakusa
calendar.area: Otori Shrine & Chokoku-ji, Asakusa/Senzoku, Taito
calendar.cost: Free entry; kumade from ~JPY 1,000.
calendar.bookingNotes: No booking. Fuchu's Okunitama Shrine also holds Tori-no-Ichi on the same rooster days if you want a quieter, suburban alternative. ⚠️ Typhoon/rain can postpone or cancel — check the official site the day before.
calendar.why: Second and final rooster-day fair of 2026 (no third this year). If you missed Nov 7, this is your other chance for the kumade market and night-stall atmosphere. Same midnight-to-midnight run.

calendar.title: Hachioji Ginkgo Festival (Icho Matsuri) — Koshu Kaido golden road
calendar.area: Koshu Kaido (Oimachi–Takao), Hachioji, western Tokyo
calendar.cost: Free; food/craft stalls cheap.
calendar.bookingNotes: No booking. Held mid-to-late-Nov weekend; 2026 dates Nov 21–22 per current listings — verify closer. Pair with a Takao hike earlier the same day. ⚠️ Typhoon/rain can postpone or cancel — check the official site the day before.
calendar.why: Community-led festival under 700+ ginkgo trees lining a ~4km stretch of the old Koshu Kaido highway. Local food/craft vendors, a classic-car parade, stamp rally — lively, low-tourist, very local. Easy to combine with Mt. Takao on the same Keio line.

calendar.title: Tokyo Festival of Modular 2026 (modular synth festival)
calendar.area: Daikanyama area, Tokyo (recent editions at ORD, ~4 min from Daikanyama Stn)
calendar.cost: Day/2-day passes typically ~JPY 3,000-6,000 (varies by year/tier); workshops/concerts may price separately.
calendar.bookingNotes: Exact 2026 dates/venue/price unconfirmed — 'late November' is the best estimate; verify closer via the official FoM Facebook/site. Tickets sell via the festival site once announced. Smaller boutique event, so it can sell out — watch their social channels.
calendar.why: Direct match for the music-gear/synths interest: Japan's main modular-synth gathering with Eurorack makers (e.g. Erica Synths), workshops, and live electronic sets — also a built-in synth community to plug into.

calendar.title: Tokyo Festival of Modular (FoM)
calendar.area: Daikanyama (concerts at ORD ~4 min from Daikanyama Stn) + exhibitor space
calendar.cost: Day/weekend passes (~¥3,000–6,000); free-ish exhibitor browsing varies
calendar.bookingNotes: Annual, late November · Advance tickets via festival site / Peatix; verify dates closer
calendar.why: Japan's flagship modular-synth gathering — hands-on gear from makers, performances and workshops. The single best event for the modular/maker lane. Verify exact 2026 dates closer.

calendar.title: Shibuya Blue Cave (Ao no Dokutsu) Illumination
calendar.area: Shibuya (Yoyogi Park / NHK / Koen-dori)
calendar.cost: Free
calendar.bookingNotes: Walk-up, free (this is the original Shibuya street version, NOT the paid Yomiuriland one). ~250m of zelkova trees in blue LED with a mirror sheet down the center. Very crowded weekends - go a weekday evening. DATE NOTE: recent years run early-to-late December (first week to ~Dec 27/31). Exact 2026 dates unconfirmed - verify closer (announced ~Nov 2026).
calendar.why: Japan's largest blue illumination - a saturated, surreal blue tunnel near Yoyogi Park, walkable from Shibuya nightlife. Free and central.

calendar.title: MUTEK.JP 2026 (Edition 11)
calendar.area: Shibuya, Tokyo — evening shows at Spotify O-East & WWW; daytime at Shibuya venues
calendar.cost: Day passes / full passes (~¥4,000–12,000)
calendar.bookingNotes: Mid–late Nov 2026, 3–4 days (2025 ran Nov 21–23; verify exact 2026 dates closer) (Dec 10–13, 2026) · Official site / Peatix
calendar.why: The city's premier festival for audiovisual and experimental electronic performance — exactly the cutting-edge synth/AV programming this lane lives for. Verify 2026 dates closer.

calendar.title: Jump Festa 2027 (Shueisha / Weekly Shonen Jump festival)
calendar.area: Makuhari Messe, Chiba
calendar.cost: General admission is free, but entry uses a free advance-reservation/lottery system; some paid priority/early-entry and stage tickets exist.
calendar.bookingNotes: Dates are an estimate — Jump Festa is held the weekend before Christmas, so Dec 19-20, 2026 is the likely window; Shueisha had not confirmed as of mid-2026. Free entry but you must register/win a free timed-entry slot in advance (lottery opens ~Nov); stage events and merch lines need separate tickets/lottery. Verify dates and reservation window on the official Jump Festa site (~Sept-Oct).
calendar.why: Japan's largest manga/anime publisher festival — huge for Jump franchises (One Piece, Dragon Ball, etc.), game reveals, and stage shows; matches anime/games interest and falls neatly before the year-end Comiket.

calendar.title: Comiket C109 (Winter Comic Market) — special 3-day edition
calendar.area: Tokyo Big Sight, Ariake, Koto-ku, Tokyo
calendar.cost: Paid timed-entry wristband via Comiket WebCatalog, tiered ~JPY 1,000-5,000+ by entry slot; limited same-day sales. WebCatalog ~JPY 1,000.
calendar.bookingNotes: CRITICAL booking: same WebCatalog wristband system as summer. First 3-day Comiket since C97 (2019). Ticket on-sale opens roughly a month out (late Nov / early Dec) — buy early-morning slots immediately, they sell out. Overlaps the New Year holiday, so plan transit/accommodation early. Verify exact on-sale dates/prices at comiket.co.jp.
calendar.why: Year-end edition of the world's biggest doujin event, expanded to three days — flagship otaku experience and a strong way to close out the calendar year in Tokyo.

calendar.title: New Year's Eve Countdown - Zojoji Temple bell ringing (joya no kane) under Tokyo Tower
calendar.area: Shiba Park / Minato
calendar.cost: Free
calendar.bookingNotes: Free walk-up. Crowds gather from ~10pm; monks ring the bell 108 times near midnight with Tokyo Tower lit directly behind. White-balloon release at midnight in some years (capacity-limited - arrive early). NOTE: the Shibuya Scramble Crossing street countdown has been cancelled in recent years and the Hachiko statue boxed off - do NOT plan a Shibuya street countdown. Balloon release not guaranteed - verify closer.
calendar.why: Most photogenic, traditional Tokyo NYE: 108 bell strikes (casting off worldly desires) framed by Tokyo Tower. Free, central, a real oshogatsu experience rather than a club night.

calendar.title: OHJO Rave NYE
calendar.area: Shinjuku Kabukicho, Tokyo — OHJO Bldg ('red-brick castle of Kabukicho')
calendar.bookingNotes: NYE overnight, 31 Dec 2026 → 1 Jan 2027 (2025→26 ran ~10pm–late) · RA / door
calendar.why: Multi-floor Kabukicho NYE rave — a fun, central, all-night way to bring in the new year on the dance floor. Verify 2026 edition closer.

calendar.title: Hatsumode at Meiji Jingu (first shrine visit of the year)
calendar.area: Harajuku / Yoyogi
calendar.cost: Free (small offering; ~¥5 coin is lucky)
calendar.bookingNotes: Free walk-up, no ticket. Japan's #1 hatsumode site - ~3 million visitors over Jan 1-3. Gates open ~6:40am NYE through ~6:30pm Jan 1, then ~6:40am-6pm Jan 2-3. To dodge worst queues: go very early (~before 8am) or visit Jan 2/3 instead of Jan 1. Dress very warm - long outdoor waits. Exact 2027 gate hours mirror prior years - verify closer.
calendar.why: The quintessential Japanese New Year ritual at Tokyo's grandest Shinto shrine, a short walk from Harajuku. Bucket-list cultural experience for a yearlong resident.

calendar.title: Hatsumode at Senso-ji (Asakusa)
calendar.area: Asakusa
calendar.cost: Free
calendar.bookingNotes: Free walk-up. Streets around Nakamise are blocked off for crowd control. For a milder crowd, join joya no kane on the evening of Dec 31, or go early morning / after Jan 3. Combine with food stalls and omikuji fortunes. Specific crowd-control timing verify closer.
calendar.why: Tokyo's oldest temple and a top-tier hatsumode spot with classic festival-stall atmosphere. Different vibe from Meiji Jingu (Buddhist temple + old-Tokyo street energy).

calendar.title: Hatsuuri & Fukubukuro (New Year first-sale lucky bags)
calendar.area: Citywide (Shinjuku, Ginza, Ikebukuro, Akihabara, Shibuya)
calendar.cost: Lucky bags typically ¥3,000-¥30,000+ (value usually exceeds price)
calendar.bookingNotes: Most department stores open Jan 1 or Jan 2; electronics (Yodobashi/Bic in Shinjuku/Akihabara) and many shops open Jan 1. Popular brand/hobby/anime bags (Starbucks, Mister Donut, apparel, synth/gear shops, anime goods) often need online reservation in late Nov-mid Dec or sell out within minutes in-store - reserve early if targeting a specific brand. Arrive at opening; queues form before dawn for hot bags. Specific 2027 reservation windows open ~Nov 2026 - verify closer.
calendar.why: Directly hits the music-gear, games and anime interests - electronics and hobby retailers do fukubukuro. Genuinely good value and an only-in-Japan New Year ritual.

calendar.title: Tokyo Disney Resort New Year's Program (Disneyland & DisneySea)
calendar.area: Maihama / Urayasu (Chiba)
calendar.cost: 1-Day Passport ~¥7,900-¥10,900 (date-priced); NYE countdown ticket separate, lottery/very limited
calendar.bookingNotes: Buy dated passports online in advance (date-based pricing; sells out on holidays). The Dec 31 -> Jan 1 countdown event with midnight fireworks is a separate special ticket, usually by lottery - apply early if you want it. New Year program (characters in kimono/New Year attire, special menus/merch) runs ~Jan 1-11. Exact 2027 dates + countdown lottery details verify closer (announced ~autumn 2026).
calendar.why: Covers the Tokyo Disney interest with a uniquely Japanese twist - characters in New Year kimono. Mid-Jan to mid-Feb is also Disney low season (shorter waits) if you skip the holiday peak.

calendar.title: Snow day-trip / ski: GALA Yuzawa (Shinkansen from Tokyo Station)
calendar.area: Yuzawa, Niigata (day trip from Tokyo)
calendar.cost: Joetsu Shinkansen round-trip ~¥13,000-14,000 + lift/rental; discounted JR EAST 'GALA day trip' packages available
calendar.bookingNotes: ~75-77 min from Tokyo Station on the Joetsu Shinkansen straight into the resort building (only ski resort with its own Shinkansen station). Rentals, lessons, lockers all on-site - ideal for beginners and non-skiers (snow play + onsen). Book Shinkansen seats / JR EAST online package a few days ahead, especially weekends. Yuzawa town has many onsen to soak after. WHV tip: as a resident you can wait for a clear-weather weekday for cheaper, quieter slopes. Season typically ~mid-Dec to early May; confirm operating dates/snow conditions closer.
calendar.why: The single easiest snow + onsen day trip from Tokyo - bullet train door-to-door, no car, no overnight. Perfect winter escape between illuminations and New Year.

calendar.title: Onsen day-trip option: Hakone / Kusatsu / Nikko (snow + hot springs)
calendar.area: Hakone (Kanagawa) / Kusatsu (Gunma) / Nikko (Tochigi)
calendar.cost: Hakone Free Pass ~¥6,100 (2-day) from Shinjuku; Nikko ~¥2,800+ rail one-way; Kusatsu ~3.5-4 hrs each way (best overnight)
calendar.bookingNotes: Hakone: easiest onsen day trip (Romancecar from Shinjuku), Mt Fuji views on clear winter days, Hakone Free Pass covers train/cablecar/boat. Nikko: snow-blanketed Toshogu shrines, day-trippable on the Tobu line. Kusatsu: famous yubatake hot-spring field - doable as a long day but better overnight. Check winter road/cablecar closures and weather. Onsen towns open year-round; the date is a placeholder for a flexible winter weekend - verify winter transport/cablecar closures closer to travel.
calendar.why: A flexible, lower-effort alternative to skiing for the onsen side of winter - hot springs with snowy scenery. Hakone pairs Fuji views with bathing for a perfect cold-weather reset.

calendar.title: Setsubun bean-throwing (Senso-ji, Zojoji, and shrines citywide)
calendar.area: Asakusa (Senso-ji), Shiba (Zojoji), citywide
calendar.cost: Free to watch
calendar.bookingNotes: Free walk-up. Senso-ji draws ~100k (chants 'Senshu banzai, fuku wa uchi'); Zojoji holds one of Minato's largest gatherings at Tokyo Tower's base, ~noon-1pm, often with celebrity guests. Try to catch a thrown fukumame (lucky bean) packet. Eat an ehomaki sushi roll facing the year's lucky direction. Setsubun is Feb 3 in 2027 (fixed); individual temple program times verify closer. ⚠️ Typhoon/rain can postpone or cancel — check the official site the day before.
calendar.why: Lively, free, very local seasonal festival marking the end of winter - great cultural moment, easy to attend at multiple iconic temples.

calendar.title: Setagaya Plum Festival at Hanegi Park
calendar.area: Setagaya (Umegaoka, Odakyu line)
calendar.cost: Free
calendar.bookingNotes: Free walk-up. ~670 plum trees in a residential-area park; weekend food/craft stalls, koto music, tea ceremony during the festival. Less touristy than Yushima - a more local, relaxed plum-viewing. Annually early Feb - early Mar; exact 2027 dates verify closer (~Jan 2027). Bloom timing weather-dependent.
calendar.why: One of Tokyo's largest plum groves and a low-key local festival - good for a peaceful weekend off the tourist track on the west side.

calendar.title: Yushima Tenjin Plum Blossom Festival (Bunkyo Ume Matsuri)
calendar.area: Bunkyo (Yushima / Ueno area)
calendar.cost: Free (shrine grounds)
calendar.bookingNotes: Free walk-up. ~300 plum trees (many white Shirokaga). Peak usually second half of February. Weekend stage events/tea service during the festival. Yushima Tenjin is the shrine of learning - busy with exam-season prayers too. Festival annually early Feb - early Mar; exact 2027 dates announced ~Jan 2027. Bloom timing weather-dependent - verify closer.
calendar.why: Plum (ume) blooms before cherry blossoms - the first real sign of spring, a quieter, fragrant flower-viewing season. Central, free, classic Tokyo winter-into-spring outing.

calendar.title: Plum blossom (ume) season — Yushima Tenjin Bunkyo Ume Matsuri
calendar.area: Yushima / Bunkyo (also Setagaya, Kameido Tenjin, Hanegi Park)
calendar.cost: Free (Yushima Tenjin grounds); some events free, plum-wine tasting/food paid
calendar.bookingNotes: Walk-up. No reservation needed. Festival runs roughly early Feb to early March every year; peak bloom mid-Feb to early March. Yushima Tenjin has ~300 plum trees plus nighttime illumination to ~19:30. Weekend shamisen/dance demos.
calendar.why: Ume kicks off Tokyo's flower season ~6 weeks before sakura and is far less crowded — a calm warm-up to hanami and a great photography target during your first quiet weeks in the city. Yushima is a short walk from Ueno.

calendar.title: Wonder Festival 2027 [Winter] (figures & garage kits)
calendar.area: Makuhari Messe, Chiba
calendar.cost: Advance Guide Book/ticket ~JPY 2,500-3,500 (the Guide Book is the entry pass).
calendar.bookingNotes: Reported Feb 8, 2027 (one-day Sunday, 10:00-17:00) — verify on the official wonfes.jp site. Buy the advance Guide Book before the event for entry and access to limited one-day garage kits.
calendar.why: Winter edition of Japan's top figure/garage-kit festival; pairs naturally with the anime/figure-collecting interest and fills the Feb gap in the convention calendar.

calendar.title: Ueno Sakura Matsuri — Ueno Park cherry blossom festival
calendar.area: Ueno Park, Taito
calendar.cost: Free (park entry); food stalls and museums paid
calendar.bookingNotes: Festival window estimated (2026 ran ~Mar 14–Apr 5); peak viewing late March. Walk-up, no reservation. ~1,000 trees along the central path; ~800 paper lanterns lit after sunset. One of Tokyo's most crowded hanami spots — arrive early morning for tarp space. ⚠️ Typhoon/rain can postpone or cancel — check the official site the day before.
calendar.why: Classic raucous tarp-picnic hanami with food stalls, open-air antique markets, and museums (Tokyo Nat'l Museum, science museum) on-site — ideal for an all-day outing. Note: alcohol is allowed here, unlike Shinjuku Gyoen.

calendar.title: Tokyo first bloom (kaika) — sakura season opens
calendar.area: Citywide; official benchmark tree at Yasukuni Shrine, Chiyoda
calendar.cost: Free
calendar.bookingNotes: FORECAST ONLY — verify closer. First official JMA/private forecasts publish around late Jan–Feb 2027. Estimated first bloom ~Mar 23–26 based on recent-year patterns; nothing is confirmed yet. Bloom can swing ±1 week with weather.
calendar.why: Kaika is the starting gun — typically ~1 week before full bloom. Marks when to start checking daily bloom trackers (japan-guide.com, weathernews) and lock in any paid/reserved hanami plans.

calendar.title: Chidorigafuchi yozakura + rowboat rental (Imperial Palace moat)
calendar.area: Chidorigafuchi / Kitanomaru, Chiyoda (near Kudanshita)
calendar.cost: Free to walk; rowboat ¥800/30min or ¥1,600/hour; evening illumination free
calendar.bookingNotes: Dates track peak bloom (2026 yozakura ran ~Mar 26–Apr 6). Boats can be reserved in advance to skip the multi-hour queue — book the timed slot online. Boathouse closes/limits in bad weather. Evening light-up over the moat.
calendar.why: Rowing under blossom-heavy branches with petals on the water is the iconic Tokyo sakura photo. Advance boat booking is the difference between a 5-minute walk-on and a 2+ hour line at peak.

calendar.title: Meguro River (Nakameguro) yozakura — night cherry blossoms
calendar.area: Nakameguro / Meguro River promenade
calendar.cost: Free to stroll; riverside bars/food stalls paid; hanami cruise ~¥3,000–6,000
calendar.bookingNotes: Dates track peak bloom (medium confidence). 2026 cruises ran Mar 20–Apr 12 as reference; book the Meguro River sakura cruise in advance via Rakuten Travel Experiences / Tokyo Waterways. ~800 Yoshino trees, bonbori lanterns over the canal after dusk. Extremely crowded on peak weekends — go on a weekday evening.
calendar.why: Tokyo's most photogenic yozakura spot and walkable from central Tokyo. Pairs perfectly with your interest in nightlife, food stalls, and the trendy Nakameguro café/record-shop scene.

calendar.title: Shinjuku Gyoen sakura — RESERVED/PAID timed entry
calendar.area: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
calendar.cost: ¥500 adult (online prepay: credit card / PayPay / Paidy — cash NOT accepted on peak days)
calendar.bookingNotes: GOTCHA: During peak sakura weekends Shinjuku Gyoen runs a TIMED-ENTRY ADVANCE BOOKING system (10:00–16:00 on the busiest weekend days). In 2026 the booking window covered Mar 28–Apr 5, with reservations required on Mar 28, 29 & Apr 5. Expect similar dates in 2027 — verify on env.go.jp once announced (~early March). No reservation needed before 10:00 or after 16:00. Alcohol is BANNED inside; ~900 trees / 65 varieties means a longer bloom window than single-variety parks.
calendar.why: The most relaxed, manicured hanami in central Tokyo and the longest bloom window (late + early varieties). The reservation/no-cash rule is the single biggest trip-up for foreigners — handle it before peak weekend.

calendar.title: AnimeJapan 2027 — NOTE: moved to Osaka (not Tokyo)
calendar.area: Intex Osaka (public days Mar 27-28; business days Mar 29-30) — NOT Tokyo Big Sight
calendar.cost: Public-day ticket typically ~JPY 2,200-2,500 advance (timed entry); business days are trade-only.
calendar.bookingNotes: IMPORTANT change: AnimeJapan 2027 and 2028 relocate from Tokyo to Intex Osaka — this is a travel/overnight trip, not a Tokyo day-out. Public days Mar 27-28, 2027; business days Mar 29-30. Advance timed-entry tickets sell via the official site (on-sale usually ~Jan); buy early for preferred entry slots. Verify dates/prices/venue at anime-japan.jp.
calendar.why: Japan's largest anime industry expo (~150,000 visitors) with studio booths, stages and announcements — top-tier for the anime interest. Flagging the Osaka move so the traveler budgets shinkansen + a night rather than expecting Tokyo.

calendar.title: Tokyo full bloom (mankai) peak — best hanami window
calendar.area: Citywide
calendar.cost: Free (most parks)
calendar.bookingNotes: FORECAST ONLY — verify closer. Best-estimate peak ~Mar 29–Apr 3 (Tokyo often peaks ~Mar 30). Full bloom lasts only ~4–7 days, then petal-fall. Book restaurants/cruises/picnic supplies once the late-Feb forecast firms up; popular cruises and Shinjuku Gyoen slots sell out.
calendar.why: This is THE hanami week. Treat all other sakura items below as clustering inside this window. Build flexibility — if forecasts shift, your peak-viewing days shift with them.

calendar.title: Great Wisteria Festival — Ashikaga Flower Park (day trip)
calendar.area: Ashikaga, Tochigi (~70 min from Tokyo via Tobu/JR to Ashikaga Flower Park stn)
calendar.cost: Admission ¥400–¥2,100 (DYNAMIC pricing — set daily by bloom stage; highest at peak wisteria); illumination evening tickets separate
calendar.bookingNotes: 2027 dates estimated from 2026 (Apr 11–May 20, illumination Apr 18–May 20); verify on ashikaga.co.jp when published. Buy timed e-tickets via Rakuten Travel / Klook to skip lines on peak weekends. Peak of the 160-yr-old great wisteria + 80m white tunnel usually late Apr to early May — overlaps Golden Week, so go on a weekday.
calendar.why: Surreal cascading-wisteria tunnels and nighttime illumination — one of Japan's most striking flower spectacles and an easy solo day trip. Bridges the gap between sakura and rainy season.

calendar.title: Golden Week 2027 — peak domestic travel + price/crowd surge
calendar.area: Nationwide (affects all Tokyo activity, transport, Disney)
calendar.cost: Surge pricing on flights, shinkansen, hotels; many things sell out
calendar.bookingNotes: Confirmed national holidays: Apr 29 (Showa Day, Thu), May 3 (Constitution Day, Mon), May 4 (Greenery Day, Tue), May 5 (Children's Day, Wed). With one day of leave (Apr 30 + the May 1–2 weekend) it becomes a clean ~7-day break. Book ANY domestic travel / Tokyo Disney / popular restaurants 2–3 months ahead.
calendar.why: Biggest planning gotcha of your spring: prices spike and reservations vanish. Strategy — either travel domestically (booked early) or stay put in Tokyo and avoid intercity trains. Tokyo Disney is brutally crowded; pre-purchase dated tickets + consider a weekday inside GW if you must go.

calendar.title: Kanda Matsuri (GRAND year — odd-year main festival)
calendar.area: Kanda Myojin Shrine; processions through Kanda, Nihonbashi, Akihabara, Marunouchi
calendar.cost: Free to watch
calendar.bookingNotes: Dates estimated (~May 8–15, main parades the weekend closest to May 15) — confirm on kandamyoujin.or.jp in early 2027. CRITICAL: Kanda Matsuri's full-scale version only happens in ODD years, so 2027 IS a grand year (2026 was the small version). Main mikoshi parades Sat/Sun. ⚠️ Typhoon/rain can postpone or cancel — check the official site the day before.
calendar.why: One of Tokyo's three great Shinto festivals, and 2027 is a rare full-scale edition. Kanda Myojin is the otaku/tech shrine literally next to Akihabara — directly on-theme for your anime/games/tech interests.

calendar.title: Sanja Matsuri — Asakusa's wild three-day mikoshi festival
calendar.area: Asakusa Shrine / Senso-ji, Taito
calendar.cost: Free to watch
calendar.bookingNotes: Held the third full weekend of May; 2027 estimated Fri May 21 – Sun May 23 (confirm with Asakusa Shrine). ~100 mikoshi, ~2 million visitors over 3 days. Sunday is the climax with the three main shrine mikoshi. Streets are jammed shoulder-to-shoulder; pickpocket awareness and you'll spot traditional tattooed bearers. ⚠️ Typhoon/rain can postpone or cancel — check the official site the day before.
calendar.why: Tokyo's most energetic, crowded, and atmospheric matsuri — drums, chanting, portable shrines hoisted through old-town Asakusa. A must-experience cultural high point of your spring.

calendar.title: Design Festa Vol.65 (Spring) — independent art & craft fair
calendar.area: Tokyo Big Sight, Ariake, Koto-ku, Tokyo
calendar.cost: Advance ~JPY 800 one-day / JPY 1,500 two-day; same-day ~JPY 1,000 one-day (based on Vol.64 pricing).
calendar.bookingNotes: Spring 2027 edition; late-May is the pattern (Vol.63 was May 23-24, 2026), so May 22-23, 2027 is an estimate — verify on the official Design Festa site. Tickets via Office Gallery Harajuku, online, or convenience stores. No lottery.
calendar.why: The spring half of Design Festa — second chance in the WHV window to catch the indie-art/maker/music-merch scene before the year wraps; cheap and walk-up friendly.

calendar.title: Tsuyu (rainy season) onset — Kanto/Tokyo
calendar.area: Tokyo / Kanto region
calendar.cost: Free (weather)
calendar.bookingNotes: JMA usually declares Kanto tsuyu ~Jun 6–11 (historical avg ~Jun 7), ending ~Jul 19–22. 2027 onset is an estimate — verify via JMA closer to date. Pack a compact umbrella + quick-dry shoes; humidity climbs fast.
calendar.why: Reframes your June: pivot toward indoor culture (Akihabara, arcades, synth/gear shops, record stores, museums, conventions) and hydrangeas, which look best in rain. Good time for focused remote-work weeks given the wet weather.

calendar.title: Hydrangea (ajisai) peak — Meigetsuin & Kamakura (day trip)
calendar.area: Kamakura, Kanagawa (Meigetsuin, Hasedera) — ~1 hr from Tokyo; also Hakusan Shrine in Tokyo
calendar.cost: Meigetsuin ¥500 (peak); Hasedera ¥400 + possible timed hydrangea-path ticket
calendar.bookingNotes: Peak is typically the 2nd–3rd week of June, shifting ±1 week yearly. Meigetsuin (the 'Hydrangea Temple', ~2,500 blooms in 'Meigetsuin Blue') and Hasedera. GOTCHA: Hasedera issues numbered timed tickets for its hydrangea path on busy days — arrive at 9:00 opening. Best on a rainy weekday afternoon (after ~16:00) for fewer crowds and richer color.
calendar.why: The signature beauty of the rainy season — blue hydrangeas glow brightest when wet. A perfect rainy-day escape that turns tsuyu's downside into its highlight, and Kamakura adds temples, beach, and good food.

calendar.title: Oedo Antique Market — Tokyo International Forum
calendar.area: Yurakucho / Marunouchi (Tokyo International Forum open plaza)
calendar.cost: Free entry; bring cash for vendors
calendar.bookingNotes: Japan's largest outdoor antique market, ~250 dealers in the Forum's open plaza. RECURRING: roughly the 1st and 3rd Sunday of most months (dates drift month-to-month and it does NOT run in August), 9:00–16:00, weather permitting. The 2026-07-05 date is the next likely session after arrival — VERIFY the exact monthly dates at antique-market.jp/english before going. Easy first-weekend outing 5 min from Yurakucho/Tokyo stations.
calendar.why: A reliable, free, recurring weekend anchor for vintage/synth/camera/ceramics hunting right in central Tokyo — perfect low-commitment social outing soon after landing.

calendar.title: Tomioka Hachimangu Antique & Flea Market (Monzen-Nakacho)
calendar.area: Monzen-Nakacho, Koto-ku (Tomioka Hachimangu Shrine grounds)
calendar.cost: Free entry; cash for vendors
calendar.bookingNotes: Antique market on the shrine grounds, ~50–120 stalls (Imari ceramics, lacquerware, vintage kimono, old tools). RECURRING: antique market on the 1st, 2nd, 4th and 5th Sundays; separate flea market typically on the 15th and 28th. 08:00–dusk, CANCELLED in rain. 2026-07-12 is an illustrative Sunday — verify the month's schedule before going. 3 min from Monzen-Nakacho Station.
calendar.why: A deeper, less touristy antiques hunt than Oedo, on atmospheric shrine grounds — good recurring weekend ritual for collectors.

calendar.title: Setagaya Boro-ichi — historic 440-year flea market
calendar.area: Setagaya (Boroichi-dori, between Setagaya & Kamimachi Stations, Tokyu Setagaya Line)
calendar.cost: Free entry; cash for vendors and street food
calendar.bookingNotes: Running since 1578 and a designated Tokyo Intangible Folk Cultural Property: 700+ vendors, ~200,000 visitors, used goods/antiques/handicrafts/daikan-mochi rice cakes. FIXED twice-yearly dates: December 15–16 and January 15–16 every year (so also 2027-01-15/16). 09:00–20:00, free. Get off at Setagaya Station, walk Boroichi-dori, exit via Kamimachi.
calendar.why: Tokyo's most historic flea market and a genuine cultural event, not a tourist trap — a must-do winter outing with reliable fixed dates you can plan around.

calendar.title: Farmer's Market @ UNU (Aoyama / Omotesando)
calendar.area: Aoyama / Omotesando (plaza in front of United Nations University)
calendar.cost: Free entry; pay vendors (produce, natural wine, street food)
calendar.bookingNotes: One of Tokyo's longest-running farmers markets: ~20+ farmers plus natural-wine and fermented-food stalls, food trucks, and frequent themed mini-events. RECURRING: every Saturday AND Sunday, 10:00–16:00, ~5 min from Omotesando Station exit B2. 2026-07-04 is just the first weekend after arrival — it runs essentially every weekend year-round.
calendar.why: A dependable, free, every-weekend social/food hub in a central neighborhood — ideal default spot to meet people, graze, and decompress on arrival weekends.

calendar.title: VENT Omotesando — weekend techno/house club nights
calendar.area: Omotesando / Minami-Aoyama (Festae Omotesando Bldg B1F)
calendar.cost: Door ~¥2,000–3,500 depending on lineup; cash + ID (20+) required
calendar.bookingNotes: Tokyo's leading minimal/deep-house & techno room with a custom sound system and regular international guest DJs. RECURRING: open Fri/Sat/Sun, ~23:00–05:00. 2026-07-03 is the first Friday after arrival — check the rolling lineup at vent-tokyo.net (or RA/Discotech) for who's playing. Bring passport; no entry without photo ID.
calendar.why: The most consistent serious-techno club in the city and an easy recurring night out for the electronic-music pillar — quality lineups nearly every weekend.

calendar.title: WOMB Shibuya — techno/house club nights
calendar.area: Shibuya (Maruyamacho)
calendar.cost: Door typically ~¥3,000–4,000 (more for headliners); cash + ID (20+)
calendar.bookingNotes: Four-floor Shibuya institution since 2000, Asia's largest mirror ball and a flagship sound system, world-class techno/house on the main floor. RECURRING: events most Fri/Sat plus special nights; ~23:00–05:00. 2026-07-04 is illustrative — check the calendar at womb.co.jp/en/event for headliners and advance tickets (often cheaper than door). Bring passport.
calendar.why: A bucket-list big-room club for the music pillar with a near-constant stream of international headliners — the counterpoint to Vent's intimate room.

calendar.title: Circus Tokyo — underground club nights (Shibuya)
calendar.area: Shibuya (near Shibuya Station, Higashi)
calendar.bookingNotes: Intimate two-floor Shibuya venue (open since 2015, sister to Osaka's Circus) known for credible house/techno/bass bookings and a strong local-DJ community — a go-to since Contact closed in 2022. RECURRING: events most weekends, ~23:00–05:00. 2026-07-11 is illustrative — see Resident Advisor (ra.co) or the venue site for the rolling lineup. Bring passport.
calendar.why: A smaller, more underground room than Womb for discovering local talent and sweaty late-night sets — rounds out the electronic-music rotation.

calendar.title: Forestlimit (Hatagaya) — experimental / listening-bar nights
calendar.area: Hatagaya / Shibuya-ku (small basement venue)
calendar.cost: Door ~¥1,500–2,500, often incl. a drink; cash
calendar.bookingNotes: Tiny experimental venue/listening bar with four giant speaker stacks; programming swings from noise and ambient to disco and bass via deep-listening residencies. RECURRING: Thursday is the regular deep-listening night, with curated parties most weekends (e.g. TOTEM TRAXX / DO SHOCK BOOZE pop-ups). 2026-07-09 is an illustrative Thursday — check forestlimit.com or RA for the actual schedule.
calendar.why: The opposite end of the spectrum from the big clubs — an intimate, adventurous space for left-field electronic and live experimental music, great for meeting the underground scene.

calendar.title: teamLab Borderless — Azabudai Hills (permanent digital art)
calendar.area: Azabudai Hills / Kamiyacho (Garden Plaza B, B1F; ~5 min from Kamiyacho Stn)
calendar.cost: ~¥3,800–4,800 adult (dynamic pricing by date/time)
calendar.bookingNotes: Permanent immersive digital-art museum (reopened Azabudai Hills, Feb 2024) with borderless rooms — 'Bubble Universe', 'Megalith Crystal Formation', etc. OPEN year-round; this is NOT a single-day event — date shown as an arrival-window anchor. BOOK timed-entry tickets in advance on teamlab.art (often sell out weeks ahead, especially weekends). Distinct from teamLab Planets in Toyosu.
calendar.why: Tokyo's flagship immersive art experience and an easy rainy-day or visitor outing — book it once early when prices/slots are good rather than waiting.

calendar.title: Mariko Mori Retrospective — Mori Art Museum (Roppongi)
calendar.area: Roppongi (Mori Art Museum, Roppongi Hills, 53F)
calendar.cost: ~¥2,000–2,500 adult (typical Mori Art Museum special-exhibition pricing); verify
calendar.bookingNotes: Major retrospective of Mariko Mori (art + science + metaphysics): ~80 works across three decades — interactive installations, sculpture, video, photography, performance. Runs Oct 31, 2026 → Mar 28, 2027 per the museum's announced FY2026 schedule. Open into the evening; ticket spans a long window so easy to fit in. Confirm exact price/hours on mori.art.museum closer to opening.
calendar.why: A landmark, long-running contemporary-art show you can catch anytime over five months — high-quality indoor culture for the winter, with city-view museum access included.

calendar.title: Art Week Tokyo 2026 (citywide galleries + free AWT Bus)
calendar.area: Citywide (55 venues: galleries, institutions, AWT Focus spaces)
calendar.cost: Many gallery shows free; some institutions ticketed; free hop-on/off AWT Bus
calendar.bookingNotes: 5th edition, Nov 4–8, 2026: a record 55 venues (32 galleries, 13 institutions, 10 AWT Focus spaces) linked by a free hop-on/hop-off AWT Bus (10:00–18:00, new express lines). Collaboration with Art Basel. Plan a route in advance via artweektokyo.com; the bus makes gallery-hopping across Roppongi/Tennozu/Ginza practical.
calendar.why: The single best window to plug into Tokyo's contemporary-art scene in one concentrated stretch — free transport between dozens of galleries makes it efficient and social.

calendar.title: Roppongi Art Night 2026 (all-night art festival)
calendar.area: Roppongi (Roppongi Hills, Mori Art Museum, Tokyo Midtown, streets)
calendar.cost: Mostly free outdoor/street program; some indoor venues ticketed
calendar.bookingNotes: All-night art festival returning for the first time in 3 years, Oct 31 – Nov 1, 2026, with art/music/video/performance scattered through Roppongi from dusk till dawn (2026 RAN Focus theme: France / Nuit Blanche). Overlaps the same weekend as the Mariko Mori opening and just before Art Week — you can stack all three. Crowded; plan a loose route.
calendar.why: A rare dusk-to-dawn art takeover of a whole district — a uniquely Tokyo night out that pairs perfectly with the late-night/club rhythm you're after.

calendar.title: Tokyo Comic Con 2026 (Makuhari Messe)
calendar.area: Makuhari Messe, Chiba (Keiyo/Musashino Line to Kaihimmakuhari)
calendar.cost: Day tickets ~¥3,000–5,000+ (photo/autograph ops priced separately); buy advance
calendar.bookingNotes: US-style comic con fusing American + Japanese pop culture: movie props, celebrity guests, cosplay, exclusive merch. Dates Dec 11 (Fri) – Dec 13 (Sun), 2026 at Makuhari Messe. Buy tickets in advance on tokyocomiccon.jp; celebrity photo-ops and autographs sell out and are ticketed on top of admission. Note this is in Chiba, ~40–50 min from central Tokyo.
calendar.why: A different flavor from Comiket/TGS — Western-meets-Japanese fandom with guest celebrities and props, slotting neatly into the geek/anime pillar in mid-December.

calendar.title: Kawagoe Festival (Kawagoe Matsuri) — day trip
calendar.area: Kawagoe, Saitama ('Little Edo' Kurazukuri warehouse district)
calendar.cost: Free to watch; train ~¥500–700 each way + food
calendar.bookingNotes: UNESCO-listed float festival (Oct 17–18, 2026): ornate multi-tiered floats with dolls paraded through the historic warehouse district, climaxing in 'Hikkawase' float-vs-float musical face-offs after dark. DAY TRIP: ~30 min from Ikebukuro on the Tobu Tojo Line, or ~50 min from Shinjuku via JR. Draws ~1 million over two days — go early, expect heavy crowds; verify dates on kawagoematsuri.jp.
calendar.why: An accessible, spectacular traditional festival just outside Tokyo with a great old-town setting — a strong autumn day-trip that isn't another Tokyo fireworks show.

calendar.title: Ichigo-gari (strawberry picking) — winter day trip
calendar.area: Greenhouse farms in Chiba / Saitama / Tochigi (1–2 hr from Tokyo)
calendar.cost: ~¥2,000–3,000+ adult for 30–40 min all-you-can-eat (varies by date/farm)
calendar.bookingNotes: All-you-can-eat strawberry picking in heated greenhouses — peak sweetness is Jan–March. RECURRING SEASON, not a single date: 2027-01-16 is an illustrative weekend; the season runs ~late Dec → May. Farms cluster in Chiba, Saitama and Tochigi, most reachable by train as a day trip. RESERVE ahead (official site or a booking service) — popular farms and weekends fill up and may refuse walk-ins. Verify each farm's open dates and price.
calendar.why: A quintessential cozy winter day trip out of the city — cheap, social, indoors-warm, and easy to pair with an onsen or a small-town wander.

homeNotes: You're a Canadian citizen departing from Seattle — your WHV is already in hand, so pre-departure is logistics, not eligibility.

Cross-border tax is genuinely complex here (Canadian citizen, recent US resident, now leaving for Japan): you may have US and/or Canadian filing duties plus a Canadian departure-tax/residency question. Washington has no state income tax. Verify with a cross-border accountant — don't guess.
Keep a US mailing address and a working US bank/card while abroad; set USPS mail forwarding or a mail-scanning service before you fly.
Carry your CAD proof-of-funds statement and your status packet through Narita even though the visa is granted — immigration can spot-check.
Your Canadian passport and consular protection still run through the Embassy of Canada in Tokyo (see the Emergency tab).
arrivalSequence: Land at Narita (NRT) → receive your residence card (在留カード) at immigration.
Move into your share house (instant registrable address — no key money or guarantor).
Within 14 days: register your address (住民票) at your ward office.
Same visit: enroll in National Health Insurance + pension.
Open a Japan Post Bank (ゆうちょ) account — the most foreigner-friendly for new arrivals.
Set up phone/eSIM data + bridge spending with your Chase Sapphire Preferred (no-FX Visa) until local payment is sorted.

steps: Land at NRT. Activate your pre-installed eSIM the moment you're off the plane (no queue) — only hit an airport SIM counter as a fallback.
At immigration: present passport + WHV visa, collect your Residence Card (在留カード) on the spot. Confirm it printed correctly.
Have your status packet ready in case of spot-check: bank statement (proof of funds), accommodation booking, onward/return ticket.
Withdraw your first JPY at a Seven Bank (7-Eleven) ATM in arrivals — skip the exchange counters.
Confirm Mobile Suica is loaded in Apple Wallet (topped up from your Chase Sapphire Preferred — test foreign-card top-up early); tap into the Narita Express / Keisei toward Tokyo.
Check into your pre-booked hotel/hostel near a hub (Shinjuku/Shibuya/Ueno). Rest — don't try to do admin today.
Recover from jet lag; orient to your neighborhood. Locate the nearest konbini, ward office, post office, and station charge machines.
If you applied to your Sakura House room before you flew, confirm move-in logistics and view the room in person if possible.
Optional fun anchor: teamLab Planets Summer Sunflower Exclusive opens today — book a timed slot via DMM/Klook if you want a low-effort first outing.
Buy a cheap data top-up if needed; verify your Mobal/passport-only SIM number works for SMS.
Lock your fixed address (move into the share house or confirm your lease). The address is the gateway to everything else.
Top up your Suica buffer (¥2,000-3,000) at a station charge machine so you never get gate-stopped.
Scout your ward office location and hours for tomorrow's registration run; gather passport + Residence Card.
Note: as a Working Holiday resident you pay the full 10% consumption tax — there is no tax-free shopping for you, so don't postpone big purchases waiting for a discount that won't apply.
WARD OFFICE DAY (go early, mornings are quieter). File the Moving-In Notification (転入届); they print your address on the Residence Card's back.
Same visit: enroll in National Pension (Category-1) AND file the exemption (免除) in one motion; enroll in National Health Insurance (NHI).
Request 2-3 copies of your juminhyo (~¥300 each) — you need them for the bank.
Confirm your My Number letter will be mailed to this address (~2-3 weeks out).
Set up PayPay with your Japanese number (residence card ready for ID verification) — your primary QR wallet.
Register Rakuten Pay/Rakuten Bank as your chosen single points ecosystem (good for gear/electronics on Ichiba).
Explore a neighborhood pillar for fun: Ochanomizu synth row, Akihabara, or Nakano Broadway — low-pressure recon, not buying yet.
Confirm your Wise recipient details are saved for fast future CAD↔JPY transfers.
Rest + logistics buffer day. Catch up on anything the ward office or jet lag pushed.
Map your likely coworking / work-café options near your base for remote-work weekdays ahead.
Plan the bank-account visit for Monday (Japan Post Bank needs Residence Card with printed address + juminhyo + Japanese phone number).
Pre-book your first quiet-weekday Tokyo Disney date (~60 days out) and set up the TDR app while you're thinking about it.
BANK DAY: open a Japan Post Bank (Yucho) account with Residence Card + juminhyo + Japanese number. No hanko needed — signature accepted.
